response.justification: While the candidate meets the educational baseline (Bachelor's + 13+ years of experience) and demonstrates strong systems engineering fundamentals as a Staff Engineer at Google, there is a critical mismatch between their primarily full-stack and web-systems background and the role's core requirements. The position demands specialized expertise in SoC/IP modeling, hardware architecture, GPU design, and AI accelerator optimization—none of which appear in the resume. This Principal-level role requires deep domain expertise in hardware systems, not general software excellence.
strengths: Proven ability to optimize high-performance systems, handling millions of requests per second at Dunnhumby with low-latency architectures
Staff-level experience at top-tier companies (Google, Meta) demonstrating technical depth and leadership capabilities
Demonstrated mastery across diverse technical stacks with strong C/C++ and systems-level programming experience
concerns: No evidence of SoC or IP modeling experience, which is listed as a core required skill for the role
Complete absence of AI accelerator, GPU architecture, or deep neural network optimization background—critical to the position
Career trajectory shows web/application development and infrastructure focus rather than hardware-level systems engineering or silicon design

key_factors.factor: No AI accelerator or deep learning optimization experience
